seo.title: AI Agent Memory Management in OpenAI Agents SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent memory management in OpenAI Agents SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Agents SDK, AI Agent Memory Management problems manifest as uncontrolled growth in agent conversation histories and persistent memory stores, leading to context overflow and degraded performance. Agents accumulate tool outputs, handoff messages, and state across multi-agent workflows without eviction policies, causing token limits to be hit during inference. This is exacerbated by the SDK's default persistent memory across agent handoffs, where specialized agents retain full conversation histories indefinitely.

howItManifests.symptom: Conversation history balloons to 100k+ tokens after 5-10 handoffs
howItManifests.impact: Agents hit context window limits, lose recent context, produce incoherent responses

howItManifests.symptom: Memory stores grow 2MB+ per conversation session
howItManifests.impact: Increased latency (5x slower inference), higher API costs, eventual OOM errors
Agents repeat analysis on same data, hallucinate from context dilution
Privacy violations, incorrect personalization, security risks from data leakage

detection.name: Monitor Agent Memory Footprint
detection.description: Track conversation history token count and memory store size per agent instance
detection.code:
from openai_agents import Agent
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()
def detect_memory_bloat(agent: Agent):
    # Check conversation history
    history_tokens = len(client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        messages=agent.conversation_history,
        max_tokens=1
    ).usage.total_tokens)
    
    # Check persistent memory
    memory_size = len(str(agent.memory_store))
    
    if history_tokens > 8000 or memory_size > 50000:
        print(f'🚨 MEMORY BLOAT: {history_tokens} tokens, {memory_size} chars')
        return False
    return True

detection.name: Trace Handoff Memory Growth
detection.description: Detect exponential memory growth during agent handoffs using SDK tracing
detection.code:
from openai_agents.tracing import get_span

def detect_handoff_bloat():
    spans = get_span('agent-workflow')
    memory_growth = []
    for handoff in spans.get('handoffs', []):
        tokens_before = handoff['memory_tokens_before']
        tokens_after = handoff['memory_tokens_after']
        growth_pct = (tokens_after - tokens_before) / tokens_before * 100
        memory_growth.append(growth_pct)
        
    avg_growth = sum(memory_growth) / len(memory_growth)
    if avg_growth > 25:
        print(f'🚨 HANDOFF BLOAT: {avg_growth:.1f}% avg growth per handoff')
        return False
    return True

detection.name: Session Memory Leak Detector
detection.description: Identify agents retaining memory across unrelated sessions
detection.code:
def detect_session_leaks(agent):
    current_session = agent.session_id
    memory_keys = list(agent.memory_store.keys())
    
    leaked_sessions = [k for k in memory_keys if not k.startswith(current_session)]
    
    if len(leaked_sessions) > 5:
        print(f'🚨 SESSION LEAK: {len(leaked_sessions)} keys from other sessions')
        return False
    return True

fixes.name: Implement Sliding Window Memory
fixes.description: Keep only last N turns of conversation history using SDK memory config
fixes.code:
from openai_agents import Agent

agent = Agent(
    name='researcher',
    model='gpt-4o',
    memory_config={
        'type': 'sliding_window',
        'max_turns': 10,
        'max_tokens': 8000,
        'summarize_old': True
    }
)

# Auto-evicts old messages and summarizes when limits hit
def before_message(agent, message):
    agent.trim_memory(max_tokens=7500)

fixes.name: Session-Scoped Memory Partitioning
fixes.description: Isolate memory by user session to prevent cross-contamination

fixes.name: Tool Output Compression Before Storage
fixes.description: Summarize tool outputs before adding to agent memory

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Memory Management Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as an output guardrail in OpenAI Agents SDK to automatically detect memory bloat patterns across all agent interactions. It monitors token counts, handoff growth rates, and cross-session contamination in real-time, blocking bloated responses before they propagate. Vex's ML-powered guards learn your normal memory patterns and alert on anomalies, while auto-applying fixes like truncation or summarization.

The integration adds <1ms latency while catching 95% of memory issues before user impact. Vex traces full handoff chains, providing memory heatmaps and automated remediation workflows that roll back bloated states and apply optimal eviction policies.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install openai-agents vex-sdk

from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail
from openai_agents import Agent

# Add Vex guardrail to every agent
vex_guard = VexGuardrail(
    guardrail_id='agent-memory-management',
    rules={
        'max_history_tokens': 8000,
        'max_handoff_growth': 20,
        'session_isolation': True,
        'tool_output_compress': True
    }
)

researcher = Agent(
    name='researcher',
    model='gpt-4o',
    output_guardrails=[vex_guard]
)

# Vex auto-detects and fixes memory issues
response = researcher.run('Analyze Q3 sales data')

# Vex dashboard shows: Blocked 3/10 responses due to memory bloat
